methodology: Newcomer Failure Prediction - Structural Code Issues

issues.title: Database Column Name Mismatch: video_id vs tiktok_video_id
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer implements video upsert logic in sync-orchestrator.ts. They notice the code uses onConflict: 'video_id' (line 158), but schema.sql defines tiktok_video_id (line 219). The upsert fails silently because 'video_id' doesn't exist as a unique constraint. Videos are duplicated in the database. Two weeks later, dashboard analytics show double-counted content metrics, and the newcomer's first contribution broke production.
issues.root_cause: sync-orchestrator.ts assumes column is 'video_id' but schema.sql defines 'tiktok_video_id'. The CrawledVideo interface (adapters/types.ts line 85) uses 'video_id', creating a mismatch. schema-v2.sql (line 115) tries to add a UNIQUE constraint on 'video_id' which doesn't exist, causing the migration to fail.

fix.option_a: Rename database column tiktok_video_id to video_id (requires DB migration)
fix.option_b: Update sync-orchestrator.ts to use tiktok_video_id (recommended, no migration)
fix.details: Line 146 of sync-orchestrator.ts: change 'video_id' field to 'tiktok_video_id'. Line 158: change onConflict constraint name. Line 115 of schema-v2.sql: fix constraint name to match schema.sql.

issues.title: Type Mismatch: commission_rate Stored as Percent (30) Not Decimal (0.30)
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer reads reminder-engine.ts line 114: commission_rate: (creator.commission_rate * 100).toString() and assumes commission_rate is 0.30 (decimal). They add a feature displaying 'Your 30% commission'. The database stores 30 (percent), so multiplying by 100 produces '3000%'. Creators see '3000% commission' and file support tickets. The newcomer spends 3 hours debugging.
issues.root_cause: Inconsistent data model: schema.sql stores commission_rate as DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 30.00 (already a percent), but reminder-engine multiplies by 100 assuming it's 0.30. The trigger (schema.sql line 330) sets values to 30, 35, 40 (percents), but code treats it as decimal. No JSDoc comment clarifies the interpretation.

fix.option_a: Change schema to store 0.30-0.40 instead of 30-40 (requires all trigger/RPC updates)
fix.option_b: Document in Creator interface that commission_rate is percent, remove multiplication
fix.details: Add JSDoc to Creator.commission_rate: 'Stored as PERCENT (30-40), NOT decimal (0.30-0.40)'. Update reminder-engine.ts lines 114, 125, 185, 196 to remove ' * 100'.

issues.title: Broken ReferralEngine Update: Query Object Passed Instead of Executed Value
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer reviews referral tracking and notices line 150 in referral-engine.ts doesn't properly increment uses_count. The code passes a query object to update() instead of a numeric value. A creator's referral code gets used 50 times but shows 0 uses. The creator complains about missing bonuses. The newcomer realizes they need to reconcile 50 referrals manually.
issues.root_cause: Line 150: update({ uses_count: this.supabase.from('referral_codes').select('uses_count') }) passes a query builder object instead of executing it. The code compiles because TypeScript doesn't catch nested unawaited chains. Error handling logs failures but the underlying logic is broken.

fix.option_b: Fetch, increment in JS, update back (race condition risk)
fix.details: Create RPC function in schema-v2.sql to atomically increment uses_count. Update line 150 to call the RPC instead of update().

issues.title: Silent Notification Failures: No Return Value Validation Before DB Update
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer adds a new notification sender but forgets to return true. The ReminderEngine (line 129) calls sendDM() without checking the result. If sendDM() returns false (API error), the shipment status is still updated to 'reminder_1' (line 138). Creators never receive notifications but the system records them as sent. Days later, creators complain they never got reminders.
issues.root_cause: ReminderEngine treats sendDM() as fire-and-forget. The interface returns Promise<boolean> but callers don't validate. Status is always updated regardless of actual send success. Email failures (missing API key, network error) are silently recorded as sent.

fix.option_a: Check return value; only update status if send succeeds (recommended)
fix.option_b: Document fire-and-forget pattern, accept silent failures
fix.details: Lines 129-131: capture dmSent and emailSent booleans. Throw error if either is false before updating DB status.

issues.title: Weak Null Guard in RecommendationEngine.computeMetrics()
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer refactors computeMetrics() to cache content elsewhere. They remove the early guard at line 114. When processing a creator with no videos, line 142 accesses content[content.length - 1] and crashes. The entire sync job fails. A week of recommendations are lost.
issues.root_cause: Guard at line 114 checks content.length === 0 but only checks for empty arrays. If content is null/undefined, or if a refactor removes the guard, array access crashes. Pattern is fragile for future maintainers.

fix.details: Line 113: change condition from 'if (content.length === 0)' to 'if (!content || content.length === 0)'.

issues.title: Middleware Admin Check Uses Cached JWT Claims, Not Real-Time Permissions
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer adds an admin-only API route and copies middleware pattern (line 62-65): if (userRole !== 'admin'). They don't realize this checks cached JWT claims, not the database. If an admin is revoked in Supabase auth, they retain access for 1 hour until token refresh. A departing contractor keeps admin access. The newcomer didn't know middleware doesn't validate real-time permissions.
issues.root_cause: middleware.ts line 62 checks session.user.user_metadata?.role, a JWT claim set at login and cached. It's not refreshed per request. Comment (line 60) says 'OR admins table' but code doesn't implement it. Newcomers assume it's checking current permissions.

fix.option_a: Query admins table in middleware (slow, repeated DB calls)
fix.option_b: Document JWT caching, require session refresh for changes (unsafe)
fix.option_c: Validate admin status in each API route, not middleware (recommended, defense in depth)
fix.details: Remove admin check from middleware. Add validation in each protected route handler that queries the admins table server-side.

issues.title: Creator API Route Computes Metrics from Unpopulated Schema Fields
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer adds a creator dashboard and copies the pattern from GET /api/creator (lines 40-45). The code calculates avgCtr from content, but schema.sql defines ctr column (line 228) but sync-orchestrator never populates it. The calculation returns 0, and recommendations fire with false advice. Creators see inaccurate metrics.
issues.root_cause: route.ts assumes content.ctr and content.cvr exist but sync-orchestrator only populates views, likes, comments, shares (lines 151-155). Computing averages of undefined fields produces NaN or 0. No error is thrown; silent degradation.

fix.option_b: Populate ctr/cvr in sync-orchestrator (requires new logic)
fix.option_c: Add guards and warning logs in route.ts (recommended, non-breaking)
fix.details: Lines 39-45: add comment noting ctr/cvr aren't populated. Add console.warn() if avgCtr === 0 despite content existing.

issues.title: Hard-Coded AI Model Version with No Central Configuration
issues.newcomer_failure_scenario: A newcomer updates the AI model version at line 68 in prompt-templates.ts from 'claude-3-5-sonnet' to 'claude-opus-4', not knowing it's also referenced elsewhere. Recommendation generation fails silently (line 102 returns empty array). A week later, recommendations are gone. The newcomer should have created a central config.
issues.root_cause: prompt-templates.ts hard-codes model name (line 68) with no central configuration. Newcomers won't know it's used elsewhere or that they should create a config file. No MODEL_VERSION constant in utils.ts or environment variable.

fix.option_a: Create AI_CONFIG constant in utils.ts (recommended, version-controlled)
fix.details: Add AI_CONFIG = { ANTHROPIC_MODEL, ANTHROPIC_MAX_TOKENS, ANTHROPIC_VERSION } to utils.ts. Import and use in prompt-templates.ts line 68.

key_findings: Two critical data type mismatches (video_id column, commission_rate interpretation) silently corrupt data with no error signals
Broken referral tracking update query produces silent failures with no validation
Notification failures are not validated before recording as sent, creating false completion records
Weak null guards throughout reminder and recommendation engines enable crash-on-empty-data scenarios
Middleware admin check uses cached JWT, creating up-to-1-hour privilege escalation window
API route assumes unpopulated database fields exist, producing false recommendation logic
Hard-coded configuration (AI model, affiliate target, keywords) scattered throughout codebase
recommendations: Fix SC-001 and SC-002 immediately: type/schema mismatches silently corrupt production data
Add return value validation to notification and referral systems (SC-003, SC-004)
Add explicit null/undefined guards before accessing array indices (SC-005, SC-007)
Move admin validation from middleware to per-route handlers with real-time DB checks (SC-006)
Centralize configuration for third-party APIs and business metrics (SC-008)
Add integration tests for sync-orchestrator → database roundtrips to catch silent failures
Document all numeric/enum field interpretations in JSDoc comments (especially commission_rate, source, status)
Create a SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS.md file documenting v1/v2/v3 versions and which features require which
